ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-03-2025 Continued To Jun-10-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And Discovery Stay
Off Calendar Feb-07-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. JEFFREY WINDSOR, Esq. of LLW Ventures LLC (tel: 650-638-2360) for Plaintiff.
Atty. JAMES GIACCHETTI, Esq. (Tel: 669-226-8605) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS MICHAEL YIN, JEFFREY DRAEGER, MARCELLA MANCE, AND PROMAX BUILDERS, INC.'s Motion For Protective Order And Discovery Stay.

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications:

Defendants' motion for protective order and stay is denied without prejudice.

A party seeking a protective order must show good cause for issuance of the order by a preponderance of evidence. (Stadish v. Sup.Ct. (Southern Calif. Gas Co.) (1999) 71 CA4th 1130, 1145.)

Defendants argue that the propounded discovery calls for production of broad categories of Defendants' and unnamed third parties' personal, corporate, financial, commercial, contractual, and business records that are irrelevant, and notes privacy and other rights are implicated. (Mot., 9:7-10.) Plaintiff argues the RFPDs solely seek information in support of the allegations in the Complaint and do not call for financial condition discovery. (Opp. 13:2-4.) Defendants' vague and conclusory statements concerning the potential for private information contained in documents fail to demonstrate good cause for a protective order by a preponderance of the evidence.

Counsel for the Plaintiff is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the tentative ruling with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants To Produce Code-Compliant Discovery Responses, To Produce Documents, And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-07-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF LLW VENTURES LLC's Motion To Compel Defendants To Produce Code-Compliant Discovery Responses, To Produce Documents, And Request For Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with a modification:

Plaintiff's motion is denied for failure to comply with CRC 3.1345. Parties are ordered to meet and confer by February 28, 2025, regarding the discovery issues raised in the above motions. Plaintiff's deadline to file a further motion to compel is extended until March 17, 2025. Sanctions are generally mandatory when a party unsuccessfully makes or opposes a discovery motion unless the party acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make the imposition of sanctions unjust. The court finds that both parties acted without substantial justification here and does not award sanctions.

Counsel for the Plaintiff is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the tentative ruling with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
